title: Ce facem dacă ce a început Trump continuă și fără el?
summary: The article examines whether Trump's purely transactional approach to foreign policy represents a permanent shift in international relations rather than a temporary anomaly. It questions whether Romania and other states have the institutional capacity, skilled negotiators, and real leverage to survive in a world where diplomacy is replaced by raw power dynamics and constant renegotiation.
talking_points: Trump has dismantled the post-WWII international order by treating states as assets rather than partners, withdrawing from 66 international organizations and ignoring international law
Security is no longer a stable commitment derived from shared values but a commodity constantly renegotiated based on immediate leverage and power dynamics
Romania faces a practical crisis: it lacks experienced global negotiators and risks having its national interest hijacked by personal interests if businesspeople enter politics
The real question is not whether Trump is an anomaly but whether the world is prepared to accept this style of leadership as the new norm
Values-based foreign policy loses real power in a transactional world — values may remain in discourse but no longer determine direction
author: Marius Vasiliu
